final_commit_sha_note: This tracked evidence file cannot contain its own final commit SHA; the final JSON response reports the final commit.
summary: Silent no-op successes were removed from request_changes_review, comment_review, and close_review. After authorization, those actions now return explicit task-contract errors because the allowed but-api/but_forge surface has no downstream write behavior for review comments/request-changes, and the branch-based close contract is underspecified relative to the existing numeric-review-id update API.

requirement_results.evidence: cargo test -p but review_guard includes review_guard_reviews_write_denied: dev/unset/empty are perm.denied and no verdict is written.

cargo test -p but review_guard includes review_guard_reviewer_commit_denied_review_accepted: reviewer commit is denied contents:write and approve writes one approved local_review_verdict at feat head.

ro is still denied comments:write; authorized reviewer is not denied but comment_review returns explicit task-contract error instead of fake success because no downstream comment write behavior exists.
Governed verbs and guards exist, but request_changes_review/comment_review/close_review cannot honestly satisfy the required success behavior with the current downstream API contract.

stub_gate_evidence.finding: request_changes_review, comment_review, and close_review now return task_contract_invalid errors after authorization; drop(message) is absent.
Search found read-only comment counts/notes only; no downstream create-comment or request-changes write operation exists.
Existing update_review can update body/state by numeric review id; it does not provide the branch-based request-changes/comment contract required by GATES-004.

minimal_spec_repair: Add real but_forge/provider operations for submitting pull-request reviews, requesting changes, and creating review comments, plus tests against those operations.
Add a local persisted review-comment/request-changes model if these verbs are intended to be local/offline like approve.
Clarify close_review's selector contract: either take a numeric review id, or define branch-to-review resolution semantics before closing via ReviewState::Closed.
